rules: Always prioritize fairness, verification integrity, and anti-abuse. Avoid pay-to-win mechanics.
CRITICAL: All new code must include unit tests. Use Jest/Vitest. Test files should be in __tests__/ directories matching source structure.
CRITICAL: All API endpoints must have integration tests. Test success, failure, and edge cases.
CRITICAL: All user inputs must be validated and sanitized. Prevent XSS, SQL injection, and other attacks.
CRITICAL: All database operations must use parameterized queries. Never use string concatenation for SQL.
CRITICAL: All authentication must be secure. Use proper session management and JWT handling.
CRITICAL: All file uploads must be validated for type, size, and content. Scan for malware.
CRITICAL: All error handling must be comprehensive. Never expose internal system details to users.
CRITICAL: All mobile-first design. Optimize for mobile usability over desktop.
CRITICAL: All performance must be monitored. Ensure sub-2-second page loads and sub-500ms API responses.
CRITICAL: All security headers must be implemented. Use CSP, HSTS, and other security headers.
CRITICAL: All environment variables must be validated at startup. Fail fast if required config is missing.
CRITICAL: All logging must be comprehensive. Log all errors, warnings, and important user actions.
CRITICAL: All third-party integrations must be tested. Handle API changes and failures gracefully.
CRITICAL: All code must be reviewed before merging. No code should be deployed without review.
Use feature flags to safely test new features. No unfinished logic in production.
Maintain at least 80% code coverage. Highlight untested code and prompt for test generation if below threshold.
All features — new and existing — must have unit and integration tests. Cursor should generate or prompt for missing tests.
If core files are changed (challenge.ts, verification.ts, payouts.ts, trustScore.ts), related tests must be updated or regenerated.
Generate and maintain an end-to-end test for the core flow: user joins → verifies → gets reward.
Verification logic must support: media uploads, app timers, optional AI/manual checks. No peer reviews allowed.
Insurance logic must be strict and include proof of attempt. Limit usage and enforce rules.
Stakes are locked after challenge start. Platform earns 5% entry fee and 20% of failed stakes.
Reward pool must follow: all completers win, split failed stakes. No winner-takes-all.
Trust score must influence verification difficulty and unlock features. Cheaters are blocked.
Enable monitoring for failed verifications, reward miscalculations, and trust score anomalies. Alert on failure.
Prioritize a mobile-first UI. Layouts must be optimized for mobile usability over desktop. Avoid clunky swipeable card patterns unless they serve a clear purpose.
